audit_status.source_base: Greek Ephesians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for worthy walk, one body/Spirit/Lord/trust/baptism/Divine, gifts, body maturity, old/new human, truth in love.

verses.restored: Therefore I, the prisoner in the Lord, appeal to you to walk worthily of the calling with which you were called,
with all humility and gentleness, with patience, bearing with one another in love,
being eager to keep the oneness of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
One body and one Spirit, just as also you were called in one hope of your calling;
one Lord, one trust, one baptism;
one Divine and Father of all, the One over all and through all and in all.
But to each one of us grace was given according to the measure of the gift of Messiah.
Therefore it says: Having ascended on high, he led captivity captive; he gave gifts to humans.
Now this, he ascended, what is it except that he also descended into the lower parts of the earth?
The One who descended is also the One who ascended far above all the heavens, so that he might fill all things.
And he gave some as apostles, some prophets, some announcers of good news, some shepherds and teachers,
toward the equipping/restoration of the holy ones, into work of service, into building up the body of Messiah,
until we all arrive into the oneness of the trust and of the knowledge of the Son of the Divine, into mature/complete humanity, into measure of stature of the fullness of Messiah,
so that we may no longer be infants, tossed by waves and carried around by every wind of teaching, by human trickery, by craftiness toward the schemes of error,
but truthing in love, we may grow up in all things into him who is the head, Messiah,

from whom the whole body, joined and held together through every supporting ligament, according to working in measure of each single part, makes the growth of the body into building itself up in love.
Therefore this I say and testify in the Lord: no longer walk as the nations also walk, in futility of their mind,
having been darkened in understanding, alienated from the life of the Divine because of the ignorance being in them, because of the hardness of their heart,
who, having become callous, handed themselves over to shameless excess, into practice of every uncleanness in greed.
But you did not learn Messiah this way,
if indeed you heard him and were taught in him, just as truth is in Jesus,
to put away, according to the former conduct, the old human, being corrupted according to desires of deceit,
and to be renewed in the spirit of your mind,
and to put on the new human, created according to the Divine in righteousness/justice and holiness of truth.
Therefore, having put away falsehood, speak truth each one with one's neighbor, because we are members of one another.
Be angry and do not sin/misalign; do not let the sun set upon your provocation,
nor give place/opportunity to the adversarial one.
Let the one stealing steal no longer, but rather labor, working with one's own hands the good, so that one may have to share with the one having need.
Let no rotten word go out from your mouth, but only what is good for building up as need requires, so that it may give grace to those hearing.
And do not grieve the holy Spirit of the Divine, in whom you were sealed into the day of redemption/release.
Let all bitterness and fury and anger and shouting and slander be taken away from you, with all harmfulness.
Become kind to one another, tenderhearted, graciously forgiving each other, just as also the Divine in Messiah graciously forgave you.

translation_notes: Truthing in love preserves the verbal force of aletheuontes in 4:15: truth is practiced relationally, not merely possessed as correctness.
New human language is communal and ethical, not a rejection of embodied life.
